content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check whether a triangle is valid or not, when the three angles of the triangle are the inputs. A triangle is valid if the sum of all the three angles is equal to 180 degrees.



Input Format
 
The first line contains an integer **T**, the total number of testcases. Then **T** lines follow, each line contains three angles  **A**, **B** and **C**, of the triangle separated by space. 




Output Format
For each test case, display 'YES' if the triangle is valid, and 'NO', if it is not, in a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C representing the angles of a triangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if A+B+C equals 180, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both triples sum to 180, so both are valid triangles.
First sum is 160 → NO. The remaining three sums are 180 → YES.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the three angles, compute their sum and compare it with 180. If equal, the triangle is valid and we print YES, otherwise NO. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time check per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
